descriptive_note: The locked records use different final labels: the author foregrounds mediated testimony with unresolved third-party agency, while the AI foregrounds a self-inflicted-death account.
The evidence sets partially overlap: both include the frame, unidentified approach, dagger removal, and renewed bleeding; each also selects distinct segments.
Both mechanisms include mediated testimony and later unidentified intervention, but they foreground different parts of the pathway.
The author record marks high uncertainty; the AI record marks medium uncertainty.

author_value: The testimony is presented through a medium rather than as unmediated access to the dead samurai. In IAG-JP-017 to IAG-JP-020, an unidentified person approaches under conditions of limited visibility, removes the dagger, and is followed by renewed bleeding. These segments leave open the possibility that a third party contributed to or completed the death. The packet does not establish the medium's motive or prove deliberate deception, so the hypothesis that the medium sought to protect a killer is not treated as a supported conclusion.
ai_value: The mediated testimony presents the narrator taking the dagger before him and stabbing his own chest in one thrust. The account then adds that an unidentified someone later approached and gently removed the dagger, after which blood again filled his mouth and he sank into final darkness. The submitted label treats the self-stabbing as the primary stated death pathway while preserving the later intervention as materially relevant to the account.
descriptive_note: The rationale notes are different prose records and are compared descriptively rather than as exact text.
Both locked records preserve an alternative pathway.
The author alternative foregrounds self-inflicted injury with post-injury intervention; the AI alternative foregrounds unknown removal as causal completion.

author_value: A narrower reading is that the samurai inflicted the fatal wound himself in IAG-JP-011 and that the unidentified person merely removed the dagger afterward. Under this interpretation, the later intervention does not displace self-inflicted death as the primary causal account.
ai_value: A plausible secondary reading is that the unidentified person's removal of the dagger is not merely incidental but helps complete or accelerate the fatal outcome, since the renewed blood flow and final sinking follow immediately after that act.
descriptive_note: Both alternative notes retain later unidentified intervention as materially relevant, with different primary emphasis.

author_value: mediated testimony and unresolved third-party agency foregrounded; self-inflicted injury retained as an alternative
ai_value: self-inflicted-death account foregrounded; unknown removal as causal completion retained as an alternative
descriptive_note: Classification: substantially the same two pathways with different prioritisation. Both records retain self-stabbing and later unidentified intervention, but the primary and alternative emphasis is inversely ordered.
